why_coupled: The proof phrase is directly used to support the definition.
when_full_text_can_stay: When the proof support is short and harmless.
when_context_fill_is_better: When the proof is longer and starts polluting the definition leaf.

why_coupled: The attribution tells whose definition this is.
when_full_text_can_stay: When the whole definition-attribution span remains short and helpful.
when_context_fill_is_better: When attribution can be represented with lighter context without dragging too much surrounding material.

why_coupled: The owner explicitly says proof x attributions are especially important inside proof excerpts.
when_full_text_can_stay: When the attribution is short and directly names whose proof-backed interpretation this is.
when_context_fill_is_better: When full proof or attribution length makes one overwhelm the other.

why_coupled: Attribution can tempt the system to drag too much proof along.
when_full_text_can_stay: Rarely; only under genuinely harmless shortness.
when_context_fill_is_better: Usually, once proof is long enough that attribution no longer needs the full proof body attached.
